daily_brief: The resumption of US-Iran hostilities dominated global news on Tuesday, with fresh American strikes targeting Iranian military sites and President Trump announcing a controversial 20% toll on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz [BBC, Al Jazeera, Financial Times, Globe and Mail]. Oil prices surged above $85 as both nations vie for control of the critical waterway, while France, Germany, and the UK condemned Iran's attacks on regional targets [RFI]. In Europe, France's Bastille Day parade showcased European unity on Ukraine, with troops and aircraft from 11 Coalition of the Willing nations participating in what may be President Macron's final national day celebration [France 24, RFI]. Hungary's parliament voted to remove President Tamás Sulyok, an Orbán loyalist, using its constitutional supermajority in a dramatic consolidation of power by Prime Minister Péter Magyar's Tisza party [BBC]. Wildfires continued to ravage Fontainebleau forest near Paris, with over 1,900 hectares destroyed and 59 people arrested nationwide for suspected arson [France 24, RFI, BBC]. In the US, a federal judge voided President Trump's $1.8 billion settlement with the IRS that had granted him immunity from tax audits, referring a Trump lawyer for potential disciplinary action [BBC].

top_stories.headline: US Launches Fresh Strikes on Iran, Trump Announces 20% Toll on Strait of Hormuz Shipping
bullets: US Central Command struck Iranian air defence systems, coastal radar, missile and drone sites around Abu Musa, Bandar Abbas, Bushehr, Chahbahar, Jask and Konarak early Tuesday
Trump announced the US would 'reinstate' a blockade on Iran and charge 20% of cargo value for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, breaking with longstanding US freedom of navigation policy
Iran responded by attacking US facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, destroying radar systems in Oman, and targeting two UAE-linked tankers, killing one mariner
Oil prices rose above $84-85 per barrel, a one-month high, as the Pakistan-brokered ceasefire agreement from June appears to be collapsing

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Trump's legal disputes while covering strikes

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Pakistan's failed mediation efforts and questioned whether dialogue remains possible

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Led with market impact, oil price alarm

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted UAE threat to retaliate and potential for wider regional escalation

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Covered from Indian perspective, including MEA summoning Iranian diplomats over killed Indian sailor

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Emphasized E-3 (France, Germany, UK) joint condemnation of Iran

top_stories.headline: France's Bastille Day Parade Showcases European Military Unity on Ukraine
bullets: Aircraft from 11 European countries and approximately 500 troops from Coalition of the Willing nations participated in Tuesday's military parade in Paris
Around 30 heads of state attended, including Ukrainian President Zelensky, in what the Élysée described as demonstrating 'renewed transatlantic convergence'
The parade marks President Macron's final Bastille Day in office, intended to signal European military strength to both Russia and the US
The Kremlin dismissed the gathering as a meeting of 'warmongers' who 'do not want peace'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized European solidarity and support for Ukraine

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Included Kremlin's dismissive response, noted recent favorable battlefield developments for Kyiv

top_stories.headline: Hungary's Parliament Votes to Remove President Sulyok in Constitutional Power Shift
bullets: Prime Minister Péter Magyar's Tisza party used its two-thirds parliamentary majority to pass a constitutional amendment ending President Tamás Sulyok's term
The amendment also removes Constitutional Court head Péter Polt; both officials were seen as Viktor Orbán loyalists
Sulyok has five days to sign the amendment or refer it to the Constitutional Court; if he refers it, Magyar will launch impeachment proceedings
Opposition Fidesz party walked out before the vote, accusing Tisza of 'building a tyranny' with arbitrary power to dismiss public officials

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both the dramatic nature of the move and Fidesz's criticism that Tisza is using Fidesz's own power tactics against them

top_stories.headline: Federal Judge Voids Trump's $1.8 Billion IRS Settlement, Refers Lawyer for Discipline
bullets: US District Judge Kathleen Williams ruled the lawsuit between Trump and the IRS was filed for improper purpose and voided the settlement
The May 2026 settlement had created a $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponisation' fund and granted Trump immunity from tax audits in exchange for dropping his $10 billion lawsuit
Williams wrote the case 'was never about a party seeking judicial resolution' but rather an attempt to 'confer immunity to people and entities affiliated with the President'
A Trump lawyer was referred to state authorities to determine if ethics rules were violated

top_stories.headline: Wildfires Ravage Fontainebleau Forest Near Paris, 59 Arrested Nationwide for Arson
bullets: Over 1,900 hectares of Fontainebleau forest, a UNESCO biosphere reserve 60km southeast of Paris, have been destroyed by wildfires
A second fire erupted Monday approximately 5km from the original blaze and spread rapidly; Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez suggested it may have been intentionally set
Around 1,000 residents have been evacuated; 850 firefighters and specialized aircraft, including Canadair planes from southern France, are battling the flames
59 people have been arrested nationwide over suspected deliberate or accidental arson, including 30 adults and 29 minors; seven have been remanded in custody after admitting to setting fires

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on the potential deliberate origin and arrests

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the UNESCO heritage status and unprecedented deployment of southern firefighting resources to Paris region

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Detailed breakdown of arrests including age profiles of suspects

additional_stories.headline: ICE agent fatally shoots Colombian national during Maine immigration operation, second deadly force incident in a week
additional_stories.source: BBC World, Globe and Mail, Le Monde

additional_stories.headline: Russian anti-war politician Boris Nadezhdin detained and charged with displaying extremist symbols, barring him from September elections
Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30 as Thai authorities investigate safety lapses including locked doors and lack of emergency signage
UK signs landmark services trade deal with Switzerland eliminating roaming charges and allowing Britons to use airport e-gates
China rebukes New Zealand for supporting South China Sea arbitration ruling on 10th anniversary, calling it 'seriously undermining' to relations
Iran-backed Houthi rebels strike Saudi Arabia's Abha airport in retaliation for airstrikes on Sanaa, marking major escalation
Australian Signals Directorate warns Russian hackers linked to FSB are actively targeting critical infrastructure including defence and financial services
British whistleblower reveals US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's undisclosed business relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
India launches campaign for UN Security Council non-permanent seat for 2028-29, reaffirms support for Palestine UN membership
US Senator Lindsey Graham died from aortic rupture related to arterial hardening, medical examiner confirms
